summary: How to read accounting ledger files
content: Ledger rows use the format: date | type | amount | account | reference. Ignore comment lines that begin with #. DEBIT records money leaving the named account category. CREDIT records money entering an account or revenue being recognized. Amounts are decimal currency values and should keep two digits after the decimal. Use the reference field to connect entries to invoices, payments, payroll, taxes, or other business events.
